M-080.NAME: visible-brand-logo-integration
M-080.PATH: frontend/public/brand/krotpn-mark-96.png frontend-admin/public/brand/krotpn-mark-96.png frontend/src/components/BrandMark.tsx frontend-admin/src/components/BrandMark.tsx frontend/src/pages/Landing.tsx frontend/src/pages/Login.tsx frontend/src/pages/Register.tsx frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx frontend/src/pages/ResetPassword.tsx frontend/src/pages/VerifyEmail.tsx frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend-admin/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Login.tsx frontend/src/index.css frontend-admin/src/index.css backend/app/email/templates.py docs/plans/Phase-63.xml docs/plans/Phase-67.xml docs/plans/Phase-68.xml scripts/phase63-visible-logo-integration-smoke.mjs scripts/phase67-auth-splash-smoke.mjs scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs

H-001.text: Topology migration carries legacy VPNServer compatibility constraints

H-002.text: Routing is host-coupled — changes can mutate live system state

H-003.text: Full Tunnel routing must not reintroduce resolver precedence or split-tunneling branches

H-004.text: Removed DNS/ipset artifacts can silently drift back through stale tests or deploy docs

H-005.text: Full Tunnel cleanup is simpler than route sync but stale host-routing artifacts can break verification

H-006.text: Complimentary internal access must stay explicit or billing/analytics drift

H-007.text: Manual client issuance unsafe if scripts bypass backend provisioning

H-008.text: Device migration risky — legacy users have live peers needing deterministic primary devices

H-009.text: Handshake anomaly detection can false-positive on mobile networks

H-010.text: Device block/revoke must target one peer or operators disable whole accounts

H-011.text: Token blacklist depends on Redis — graceful degradation must not break auth

H-012.text: Alembic migrations must be backward compatible with SQLModel.create_all

H-013.text: VPN service split into 4 files — import paths must remain stable

H-014.text: Admin audit middleware must not log sensitive bodies or tokens

H-015.text: Deploy idempotency must preserve WG keys, SSL certs, .env across runs

H-016.text: Removed RU IP auto-update references must stay absent from deploy and verification surfaces

H-017.text: Changing AWG obfuscation or UDP port invalidates existing client configs unless rotation is explicit

H-018.text: Anti-abuse auto-rotation must distinguish normal roaming from true endpoint ping-pong before invalidating configs

H-019.text: Changing VPN address pools breaks existing client configs unless migration explicitly rotates/reprovisions devices; /24 pools cannot serve 500/1000 devices

H-020.text: Stale split-tunnel tests and phase references can break verification even when runtime Full Tunnel code is correct

H-021.text: Disaster recovery must use existing operator-approved deploy/install scripts as a black-box deployment surface

H-022.text: Backup bundles can expose secrets or fail restore if they omit AWG profiles, runtime env, or device/client linkage

H-023.text: Mobile user cabinet must keep config download, QR, subscription and device actions reachable without clutter

H-024.text: Mobile admin console must keep emergency operator actions visible without desktop-only dashboards

H-025.text: Session hardening must not break login/register/refresh/logout compatibility during frontend simplification

H-026.text: Email delivery must not leak verification tokens, API keys, or raw provider responses

H-027.text: Trial, VPN provisioning, referral effects, and MTProto issuance must not happen before email ownership is verified

H-028.text: MTProto assignment backup/restore must preserve SNI, credential mode, and user linkage without storing unnecessary raw secrets

H-029.text: Changing MTProto base secret or salt invalidates issued Telegram proxy links unless reissue is explicit

H-030.text: MTProto runtime bridge must degrade safely when policy loading fails and must not block normal KrotPN login

H-031.text: User dashboard must show MTProto access compactly without hiding VPN trial status or config actions

H-032.text: Shared 443 edge can break both HTTPS and Telegram proxy if fallback routing or wildcard TLS is misconfigured

H-033.text: Admin MTProto operations must never expose full secrets or proxy links in logs, tables, or audit payloads

H-034.text: Installer-provided wildcard TLS must validate cert/key/domain before deploy and must never log private key material

H-035.text: Resend API key and email verification tokens must never leak through installer, deploy, provider errors, or logs

H-036.text: Phase-41 MTProto live edge keeps RU public TCP 443 on the SNI router, routes web fallback to nginx 9443, and loads issued SNI policies without rotating existing secrets

H-037.text: DE-backed MTProto edge and RU SNI router can break HTTPS or expose policy/cert material unless routing, private API reachability, and redaction gates are strict

H-038.text: MTProto must not be shown as production-available until issued-link, public route, KPprotoN runtime acceptance, and Telegram Desktop/mobile proof all pass without leaking secrets

H-039.text: Official MTProxy runtime is planned to be superseded by Phase-41 and must not remain in the production MTProto path after rollback

H-040.text: Official MTProxy secret sync is planned to be superseded by Phase-41 and must not drive active user issuance after rollback

H-041.text: MTProto telemetry must support useful analytics without storing raw secrets, full proxy links, or unnecessary raw IP addresses

H-042.text: Runtime telemetry must be secret-free, bounded, and resilient so analytics cannot break the MTProto data-plane

H-043.text: Abuse signals start observe-only until thresholds are tuned against real mobile-network behavior

H-044.text: Admin analytics APIs must redact tags, runtime tokens, SNI secrets, full tg links, and high-risk identifiers

H-045.text: Admin analytics UI must remain compact on mobile while exposing enough filters for incident triage

H-046.text: Promotion tag control must validate MTProxyBot tags, avoid blank runtime tags, and never log full tags

H-047.text: MTProto abuse alerts must notify admins without automatically punishing users before operator review

H-048.text: Raw client IP observability is admin-only, encrypted at rest, retention-bound, and never logged or exposed to users

H-049.text: Password recovery and duplicate-email hints must not leak reset tokens, allow account takeover, or make email enumeration worse than the approved UX tradeoff

H-050.text: VPN trial activation must start from the first successful VPN handshake without locking verified users out of their first config

H-051.text: User cabinet UX polish must separate tg app links from browser links, keep MTProto secrets owner-only, and avoid stale status badges

H-052.text: CTA MTProto SNI generation must stay wildcard-safe, avoid exposing raw numeric user IDs, preserve old issued links, and not weaken RU/DE routing

H-053.text: Mobile browsers may rename .conf downloads to .txt unless the backend and frontend force attachment, octet-stream, filename, and nosniff semantics

H-054.text: BIMI sender branding depends on strict SVG Tiny P/S compliance, public HTTPS delivery, DMARC enforcement, and mailbox-provider rendering policy outside the Resend send API

H-055.text: Paid tariff catalog must keep server-authoritative prices, canonical device limits, no duplicate clean-deploy plans, and no deploy/install drift

H-056.text: Favicon and email-logo assets must remain public static branding only, while BIMI/provider avatar rendering stays a separate mailbox-provider-controlled surface

H-057.text: Matrix canvas effects must never block auth, VPN, billing, MTProto, or admin workflows; reduced-motion and canvas-failure fallbacks are release gates

H-058.text: Matrix visual redesign must stay compact and mobile-safe; neon/glitch effects cannot create one-note color, text overlap, horizontal scroll, or unreadable admin controls

H-059.text: Premium Matrix art direction must feel distinctive without becoming decorative noise or a one-note neon palette

H-060.text: Public/auth premium presentation must not delay registration, hide verification states, or imply access before email proof

H-061.text: Tablet and mobile adaptation must be verified on real breakpoint classes, not inferred from desktop layouts

H-062.text: Premium user cabinet must keep VPN config, subscription, MTProto, and device actions reachable in one or two taps

H-063.text: Premium admin cockpit must improve scanability without hiding emergency controls, MTProto redaction, or confirmation flows

H-064.text: Motion and microinteractions must respect reduced-motion, avoid scroll hijacking, and never block operational workflows

H-065.text: Premium QA gates must include screenshots, performance budgets, reduced-motion, no-overlap, and protected deploy/install guards

H-066.text: Premium Resend templates must remain token-safe, text-fallback clean, and compatible with image-blocking email clients

H-067.text: Visible logo integration must use prepared brand assets without bloating pages or conflicting with BIMI/email-avatar boundaries
